description: A hole leak vulnerability exists in the Maglev compiler's inlined Array constructor for double elements kinds, introduced in commit 006fd6637119ca51e48e33c600cb173957b76712. When Maglev inlines `new Array(x0, x1, ...)` with PACKED_DOUBLE_ELEMENTS, it calls `ConvertForField()` for the kFloat64 field type, which internally invokes `GetFloat64(value)` to convert values into float64 representation. However, unlike `ConvertForStoring()` (used in the normal keyed store path), `ConvertForField()` for kFloat64 fields does NOT call `GetSilencedNaN()` to canonicalize NaN values. As a result, if an element value has the bit pattern of `kHoleNanInt64` (the magic hole NaN sentinel used exclusively in HOLEY_DOUBLE_ELEMENTS), it can be stored directly into a PACKED_DOUBLE_ELEMENTS array via `CreateFixedDoubleArray()` -> `BuildInitializeStore()` -> `StoreFloat64` without being silenced. This allows creation of a PACKED_DOUBLE_ELEMENTS JSArray that contains what appears to be a hole at the JavaScript level, violating the invariant that PACKED (non-holey) arrays must not contain holes. An attacker can craft a JavaScript function that passes a NaN value with the hole bit pattern through the optimized Array constructor path to create a corrupted array object, which can subsequently be used to leak or manipulate internal heap values.

fixes.evidence_text: [maglev] Temporarily disable double array ctor optimization
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://crrev.com/c/7137281 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
